assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md explicitly states 'Feature-branch workflow selected based on atomicity constraints in the NFRs' and quotes both atomicity NFRs: 'Migration must be atomic' and 'All changes must land together'. Task 1 is a create-branch bookend and Task 7 is a merge-branch bookend, confirming feature-branch workflow mode.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch' and is the first task (task-1). It has Target Branch: main and Description: 'Create and push feature branch TC-9005 from main'.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch' and is the last task (task-7). It has Target Branch: main and Description: 'Merge feature branch TC-9005 to main'.

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 intermediate tasks have '## Target Branch\nTC-9005': task-2 (line 5), task-3 (line 5), task-4 (line 5), task-5 (line 5), task-6 (line 5).

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
All intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 intermediate tasks include 'Depends on: Task 1' in their Dependencies section: task-2 (line 43), task-3 (line 41), task-4 (line 40), task-5 (line 46), task-6 (line 44).

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md Dependencies section lists all 5 intermediate tasks: 'Depends on: Task 2', 'Depends on: Task 3', 'Depends on: Task 4', 'Depends on: Task 5', 'Depends on: Task 6' (lines 26-30).

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Intermediate tasks (2-6) all contain: Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (TC-9005), Description, Files to Modify and/or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Bookend tasks (1, 7) contain Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — and correctly omit Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes per the bookend task template rules. The assertion says 'at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create' and 'Implementation Notes' — bookend tasks are exempt from these per the eval instructions which state bookend tasks omit those sections.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md contains: 'Label decision: Apply the workflow:feature-branch label to feature issue TC-9005 to indicate feature-branch workflow mode.' Also repeated in the Summary Comment section.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No task file or impact map contains a description digest comment with the '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker. This is a Jira API operation that was not performed since this is an eval producing local files, not interacting with Jira. No SHA-256 hashes appear in any output file.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: The repo-backend.md fixture lists CONVENTIONS.md in the repository directory tree, but no CONVENTIONS.md content was provided as an eval fixture file. The impact map notes this gap: 'The CONVENTIONS.md file is listed in the repository structure but its contents are not available; convention-aware enrichment cannot be applied without reviewing the actual convention definitions.' No convention-aware enrichment was applied to any task, and no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' rationale lines appear. Since the assertion requires validation of file-type applicability, and no conventions content was available to validate against, this cannot be confirmed as PASS.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature TC-9005 has Priority: High. The impact map documents that priority 'High' was inherited and propagated to all tasks. However, no task file contains an 'additional_fields' section or JSON block with a 'priority' key. The assertion requires that each task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited value, which is a Jira API operation not reflected in the local markdown task files.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature TC-9005 has Fix Versions: RHTPA 2.0.0, and no ### Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md (defaulting to 'both'). The impact map documents propagation intent. However, no task file contains an 'additional_fields' section or JSON block with 'fixVersions'. This is a Jira API operation not reflected in local markdown task files.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map's 'Summary Comment (Step 6c)' section states: 'Priority "High" was inherited from the feature and propagated to all tasks. Fix version "RHTPA 2.0.0" was inherited and propagated to all tasks (fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no ### Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md).' This includes both the inherited priority and fixVersion values.
